front: Painting: Kenneth Clark called its preliminary sketch 'as free as the most vigorous Constable'

Artist: teacher who previously painted the same view at Narni before his student's famous version
Painting: silvery, shimmering landscapes of spindly trees before a lakefront, painted at a commune west of Paris where the artist inherited property

front: Artist: in his mature period, began painting landscapes from memory rather than on-site, calling them this French word

front: Artist: taught both Berthe Morisot and Camille Pissarro

Artist: over 2,100 works in the Jousseaume collection attributed to this painter were forgeries
Artist: forgers exploited gaps in this painter's catalogue raisonne by Alfred Robaut and Etienne Moreau-Nelaton
Artist: thick, diagonal lines characterize this painter's cliche-verres like Les Arbres dans la Montagne
Artist: painted an on-the-spot depiction of the Castel Sant'Angelo and St. Peter's cupola during an 1825 trip to Italy
